sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: KEPCO Accelerates West Coast Energy Highway, Eyes 2030 Completion
feed.summary: Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) is accelerating the construction of its West Coast High-Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) energy highway, aiming to complete the first phase from Saemangeum to the Seoul metropolitan area by 2030, a year ahead of schedule. KEPCO plans to dramatically shorten the typical nine-year construction timeline by streamlining procedures, completing basic design this year, and pre-ordering submarine cables. The project will transmit electricity from offshore wind farms and solar plants to the capital area, symbolizing the future direction of Korea's power grid.

feed.title: Homeowners Significantly Underestimate Cost of Home Upkeep
feed.summary: A new national study by Synchrony, "Lifetime of Home Care," reveals that homeowners significantly underestimate the lifetime costs of home maintenance and repairs, expecting to spend around $70,000 but actually facing costs over $339,000 for routine upkeep and potentially over $418,000 with emergency repairs. This disconnect creates financial stress, with many delaying essential projects and lacking sufficient reserves for inevitable emergencies. The study highlights the need for greater awareness and flexible financing solutions as repair costs rise and homes age.

feed.title: Analog Devices Strengthens Global Manufacturing Resilience with New Thailand Facility
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) has opened a new advanced manufacturing facility in Thailand, enhancing its global manufacturing resilience and expanding sustainable semiconductor production in Asia-Pacific. The facility, designed as a smart and sustainable factory, will increase ADI's test, wafer-level processing, chip scale packaging, and final IC test operations, supporting various markets. This expansion reinforces ADI's commitment to geographically diverse, agile, and environmentally responsible manufacturing, including aiming for Platinum LEED certification and using low-carbon liquid nitrogen.

feed.title: Is Citizens Financial Stock Outperforming the Nasdaq?

Despite a recent dip due to concerns about private credit exposure in the regional banking sector, analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating with a target price suggesting significant upside. CFG's stable financial position and diversified services contribute to its robust market standing.

feed.title: Will AI Ambitions and the Protectorate Deal Reshape Brown & Brown's (BRO) Acquisition-Led Narrative?
feed.summary: Barclays recently reiterated an Equal Weight rating on Brown & Brown (BRO) while trimming expectations and highlighting the potential impact of AI on the insurance brokerage landscape. This comes as Brown & Brown Dealer Services acquired The Protectorate Group Insurance Agency, blending technological change with their acquisition-led growth strategy. The article explores how this acquisition and the rise of AI could affect Brown & Brown's investment narrative and long-term earnings, especially concerning integrating new deals and managing margin pressures.

feed.title: Rhythm Awaits FDA Decision On Expanded Use Of Imcivree In Hypothalamic Obesity
feed.summary: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals (RYTM) is awaiting an FDA decision tomorrow, March 20, 2026, regarding its sNDA to expand the label of Imcivree for treating acquired hypothalamic obesity. Imcivree, also known as setmelanotide, is already approved for chronic weight management in adults and children aged two and older with specific rare genetic forms of obesity, including Bardet-Biedl syndrome, POMC, PCSK1, or LEPR deficiency. The article indicates that further details are available to premium subscribers.

feed.title: BofA raises Jabil stock price target on strong AI revenue growth

The company's AI revenue guidance of $13.1 billion for fiscal 2026 significantly beat expectations, and its efforts in liquid cooling retrofitting were completed ahead of schedule. Other analysts like Barclays and Raymond James also raised their price targets for Jabil, reflecting optimism about its AI-driven growth and performance.

feed.title: Home Depot Expands Pro Digital Workspace As Investors Weigh Valuation Gap
feed.summary: Home Depot (NYSE: HD) is enhancing its Pro digital workspace for Pro Xtra loyalty members, introducing new tools for project management, real-time delivery tracking, AI material list building, and advanced order scheduling. This strategic move aims to strengthen relationships with its high-value professional customers by addressing their daily operational pain points. Investors are closely monitoring the impact of these software-style tools on customer engagement and how they might influence the company's valuation in comparison to its peers.

feed.title: Rapid7 outlines $835M–$843M 2026 revenue guidance as AI-driven security investments continue
feed.summary: Rapid7 has provided revenue guidance for 2026, projecting between $835 million and $843 million, driven by continued investments in AI-driven security solutions. This outlook reflects the company's anticipated growth in the cybersecurity sector, emphasizing the increasing reliance on advanced technologies for threat detection and response.

feed.title: Director slate, auditor vote at GD Culture Group (NASDAQ: GDC)
feed.summary: GD Culture Group (NASDAQ: GDC) will hold its 2026 annual shareholder meeting on March 30, 2026, to vote on the election of five directors, the ratification of GGF CPA LTD as the independent auditor for the 2025 and 2026 fiscal years, and the approval of meeting adjournment if needed. The Board recommends voting "FOR" all proposals. Key leadership members like CEO Xiao Jian Wang and CFO Zihao Zhao received modest cash compensation in 2024, with prior auditor HTL International, LLC billing $180,000 for 2024 audit services.

feed.title: Six Flags Great America Chicago Reveals Plans for 50th Anniversary Celebration
feed.summary: Six Flags Great America Chicago is set to celebrate its 50th anniversary from June 20 to August 9, 2026, featuring new entertainment, limited-time offerings, and guest experiences to honor five decades of operation. Highlights include a new nighttime spectacular with a parade and drone show, a Legacy Museum, special merchandise, a reimagined Ice Cream Parlor, and live entertainment celebrating each decade. The park will open for the 2026 season on April 25.

feed.title: Chevron’s $1.5M fine for Weld County blowout will finance improvements to monitoring programs
feed.summary: Colorado energy regulators have approved a $1.5 million fine against Chevron for the largest well blowout in the state in a decade. The fine, with about $1 million dedicated to buttressing statewide enforcement programs, comes after the Bishop well incident in April 2025, which released 25,000 barrels of material and prompted evacuations. While regulators deemed the settlement "tough but fair," some environmental groups argue it does not adequately cover the damages inflicted on the community.

feed.title: Jitterbit Recognized as a Visionary in 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for iPaaS
feed.summary: Jitterbit has been recognized as a Visionary in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS), based on its completeness of vision and ability to execute with its Harmony platform. The company is noted for its leadership in integrating AI and orchestration, providing a secure and transparent system for complex integrations and autonomous agent workflows. Jitterbit's Harmony platform aims to future-proof operations and manage "agent sprawl" by offering governed AI-driven automations.

feed.title: SAB Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ: SABS) to raise $85M for SAB-142 trials
feed.summary: SAB Biotherapeutics has priced an underwritten public offering to raise approximately $85 million in gross proceeds through the sale of common stock and pre-funded warrants. The company intends to use the net proceeds, estimated at $69.7 million (or $81.7 million if underwriters fully exercise their option), primarily to fund the continued development of its lead T1D candidate, SAB-142, through ongoing and planned clinical trials, as well as related manufacturing, regulatory, and general corporate purposes. The offering includes 19,324,677 common shares at $3.85 per share and pre-funded warrants for up to 2,753,246 shares at $3.8499 per warrant.

feed.title: SAB Biotherapeutics Prices Underwritten Equity Offering; About $69.7 Million Net Proceeds Expected
feed.summary: SAB Biotherapeutics (SABS) has announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering, involving the sale of 19,324,677 shares at $3.85 per share and pre-funded warrants for up to 2,753,246 shares at $3.8499 each. The company expects to receive approximately $69.7 million in net proceeds, or $81.7 million if the underwriters' option to purchase additional shares is fully exercised. The offering is anticipated to close on March 19, 2026.
